text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. The main goal is to make the code cleaner and easier to understand. By renaming unclear variables, breaking long functions into smaller ones, and removing duplicate code, developers can boost readability. This reduces what is called "technical debt"—the hidden cost of messy code that slows down future development. Refactoring does not add new features, but it helps prevent bugs and makes the code simpler to modify later. Regular refactoring keeps a project healthy and saves time in the long run.
